_comment: Per-channel message template pools. {kw} slots are filled from the channel's keyword pool by corpus_builder.py. 'threads' are started by channel experts and replied to by others; 'questions' are asked by non-experts and answered by experts; 'chatter' is standalone filler from anyone active in the channel.

threads: Heads up team — new guidance on {kw} landed this morning. Summary in thread.
I've finished the quarterly review of our {kw} process. Three findings worth discussing.
Reminder: any new vendor touching EU data needs a {kw} before contracts go out.
Draft policy update for {kw} is ready for comments. Please review by Friday.
We got a {kw} from a customer today — walking through our response process in thread for visibility.
questions: Quick one — does {kw} apply if the data never leaves the EU?
Who can sanity-check a clause about {kw} in the Acme contract?
Is there a template for {kw} somewhere? Customer is asking.
How long does a {kw} usually take end to end?
answers: Short answer: yes, but it depends on the retention schedule. The {kw} doc in the drive covers this exact case.
I handled this last quarter — the key is documenting the lawful basis first. Happy to walk you through it.
Send it my way, I'll review it today. We updated our {kw} standard last month so use the new template.
Good question — wrote up the process here so we stop re-answering it. TL;DR: five working days if legal isn't blocked.
chatter: FYI the ICO published new enforcement stats today. Nothing alarming for us.
Renewal paperwork for our {kw} tooling is done.
Flagging that the {kw} training is due for everyone by end of month.

answers: Yeah, this bit us in March. The fix is pinning the version and clearing the cache — see the {kw} readme.
Docs are stale, the real pattern is in the checkout flow code. I'll update the docs today.
Depends on interaction density — for that page I'd skip it. Happy to pair on it.
That's mine — you need the env flag set. DMing you the setup.

Design sync moved to Thursday this week.
PSA: prettier config updated, re-run install.

questions: What's the right pattern for {kw} when the source table is append-only?
Who can grant me access to the {kw}?

answers: Use the incremental pattern with a 3-day lookback window — the {kw} doc has the exact config.
Done — you should have access now. Added you to the group rather than a one-off grant.
Historical reasons, and yes we should move it. I'll reschedule after checking the downstream jobs.
Watermark column plus a reconciliation job. I built one for events last year — reusable.
chatter: BigQuery added a new {kw} feature in preview — looks promising.
Reminder: tag your datasets with an owner or the cleanup job flags them.
Monthly data platform costs are in — trending down nicely.

threads: Q3 {kw} report is in. Two highs, both patched. Summary in thread.

How often does {kw} run, and can I trigger it manually on a PR?
Who reviews {kw} requests? Mine's been pending three days.
answers: Least privilege: start with read-only scopes and add on demand. I keep a cheat-sheet for {kw} — sharing.
Known issue with the IdP cert rotation, fix rolling out this morning. You're not locked out, just cached.
Nightly, and yes — there's a label you can add to the PR to trigger it.
That's me. Approved — sorry, the queue notification was going to the wrong channel.
chatter: This month's {kw} click-rate was our best yet. Nice work everyone.
CVE feed is noisy today, nothing affects our stack.
Badge access review happening Friday, expect an email.

questions: Why does the {kw} disagree with the finance numbers for March?
Can someone re-run the {kw} with EU traffic excluded?
What's our convention for {kw} on partner links?
Is the {kw} in the dashboard using last-click or data-driven?
answers: Timezone mismatch — the {kw} uses UTC, finance uses local. There's a reconciled view, use that one.
Done, refreshed in the dashboard. EU exclusion is now a saved filter.
Documented convention: lowercase, hyphens, source first. The linter in the link builder enforces it.
Data-driven since April. The changelog tab in the dashboard tracks every switch.

answers: There's a module for it — three lines in your terraform and you're done. Example in the platform repo.
Cache poisoning, nine times out of ten. Clear the CI cache and re-run; if it persists ping me.
Raised it. Also set up an alert so we see it before it blocks anyone next time.
Template exists as of last month — cookiecutter in the platform repo. Copying from other repos is how we got the mess.
